All right, you're back and we are ready to roll. What I've been doing here is giving you the ammunition to run this timeline regression. Essentially, what we want to do here is get into a very calm, mellow state in a private area where you're going to close your eyes and drift off into your past and go back to the moment where this limiting belief first formed. We call that your root experience. Now number one here, don't worry, I am not hypnotizing you. You are in full control the entire time. At any moment you're uncomfortable, you can simply open up your eyes and it's over. But let me just tell you something here. You're probably going to enjoy this. It's a really cool thing I'm going to do. And also, I don't want you to make this that it's so painful that you're wailing and crying and you're miserable because I've seen some people do that in seminars where they get the room crying and screaming and people are rolling on the floor because the theory is that by having that much pain, you create the impetus to change. Well, the studies have proven that it's not required at that level. Just having the area in your brain ignited, knowing what it costs you, stepping back there so you can really see yourself. You don't have to get into the worst pain possible. We need to somewhat get there, but not to the point where you're just like devastated. So we're not going to that spot. Okay? Let me just give you a quick rundown now on how this timeline regression works and then I'm going to change the location. I'm going to go to a different room sitting behind my desk and we'll take it from there. So here's what we've been doing. At one, I had you identify the limiting belief. We essentially brought it above the surface because remember the power that these limiting beliefs have is they sit below the surface and disguise themselves as facts of life. Well we demystified that belief and by doing that, we begin the process of taking away its power. We made it public. It's above the surface. And then we started saying, okay, well, how do you start? What were those experiences? Now it would be great if simply by lodging this problem out here, meaning that we logically see why this belief is costing us money, why it's not real, why we should just say, you know what, forget about it. It's not true. It would be great if simply by logic we can accomplish what we want to accomplish, which is to crack this and replace it with an empowering belief. Unfortunately, your unconscious mind is where the power lies. We have to address that at a very deep level and that my friends, what the timeline regression is all about. So again, we're going to float back in time. We're going to identify that root experience and don't worry if you don't know exactly what it is, your unconscious mind will get you there and I'll guide you through that process. Very simple to do. And then when you're back there, what I'm going to have you do is essentially float up above your timeline and say to yourself, okay, look down and what about this experience is not true? In other words, experiences are just that. They're experiences. They have no meaning inherently. See what we do is we imply meaning to an experience through the use of language. Something happens and we say, okay, because this happened, it means X. For example, husband and a wife, the wife's waiting at a restaurant sitting behind a table waiting for the other chair is empty, right? Seven o'clock meeting for dinner. He's late. 7 o'clock, five, still not there. Wife starts to, okay, she's okay. She orders a drink and gets worse, right? 7.15, he's still not there. 7.18, she's like this guy, what up? I'm telling you every time. 7.30, he walks through the door and she jumps up and throws the drink. I said, you idiot, how can you do this? You embarrass me and she walks out the door. That's that, right? Probably that happened to you at one point. Maybe you did this to someone else, right? Another meaning here, right? 7 o'clock, wife sits behind the table. Husband doesn't show up. She's getting nervous. Where's my husband? I hope something didn't happen to him. Now five, she's starting to sweat a bit and he's not really late that much and oh my God, he can't get in touch with me. He didn't call. 7.15, she's panic-stricken. 7.30, she's almost ready to cry and call the police. He walks in, she jumps up and she runs and she hugs him. Oh my God, thank God you're... Same exact experience and of course I gave you extremes on both sides, but a very different meaning. Something happens to you in your life. Good or bad? Well, it depends. What meaning did you apply through the use of language? Here's another example, business one. I went into business at the age of 21 selling meat and seafood door to door. I destroyed this business by making every mistake a young entrepreneur can make. I overexpanded, I under capitalized it, I was growing on credit. Everything you could do wrong, I did wrong. When backup lost my money and in that moment, feeling the pain of failure, I was like, you know what? Maybe I'm just not meant to be an entrepreneur. I'm just not entrepreneurial material. I'm a great salesman. Maybe that's what I should just do. Forget being a businessman. And I formed that limiting belief. And guess what? It stopped me from opening up a brokerage term for about three extra months. It wasn't until I finally started saying, you know, this doesn't really make sense that I kind of went back and said, you know, what's the real meaning? The real meaning to this experience is that maybe I should have learned the first thing about being an entrepreneur before I took a leap. You get it? See, you have an experience. You control whatever meaning you want through the use of language. And what happens is when we have limiting beliefs formed from these kind of bad experiences that have pain and catchable, typically when you're feeling pain and you're feeling disempowered in that moment, when you have a negative state, you tend to always form the wrong belief because of an improper meaning. You give the worst meaning, the disempowering meaning, and just like that, it starts. So what we're doing now, when we float back to this root experience, I'm going to have you float up so there's no longer any emotion. You know, above it, looking down, it's called being in a dissociated state. We went through this in our anchoring process, right? You look down and you see yourself moving through that root experience and say, what's the real meaning? You apply a different meaning using language. And also when you're up there floating above, you could also call out any resources you want. You could say, you know what? I need to feel more certain, some unconditional love, some confidence. And I'm going to help you get those resources. In other words, as an adult now, as a sober adult that's being trained and understands the real thing about beliefs, right? The real deal, guess what? You can look down and say, oh, I see. Yeah, I was pretty upset that day and I drew the wrong meaning. I'm going to change the meaning now. And I'm going to give myself the benefit of confidence, of courage, of clarity, of certainty, unconditional love, whatever resources you need. I'm going to show you how to grab onto them, use them in the past to change the meaning. And then once you do that, we're going to start slowly floating back up to the present and guess what happens? All you have to do is attack the root experience and as you float forward with the right language I'll give you, this is the beauty of NLP, bangles, brilliance, right? Your unconscious mind does all the work for you. See, all you have to do is get to the root experience and as you float forward, your unconscious mind will change the meanings of every experience that came after it for you. It's that powerful. Then we get to the present. I'm going to have you move into the future, which is why I had you now say, what would your life look like in the future? What things would look like? Well, now you're going to call onto those things and actually visualize them two, three years in the future and you're going to look down, see how great your life is and just trust me, we'll float back to the present and when you get back into your body, boom. You are golden. You feel like a million bucks. All right? So let me just leave you with one thought here, which is your timeline. Everyone's timeline is a bit different, meaning where does it sit in your life? Is it run through you? Is it above you? Is it kind of this way? Does it really matter? But what I want you to do now is just again, ask yourself, where is my timeline? Close your eyes real quick and point to the past. Where do you see the past? You might go whoop and you point to the past. Once you do that, say, okay, now I want you to point to the future and you might point up here. Fair enough. Whatever is true for you is true for you. Then next step is I want you to just draw a line between the distant past and the future, you know, three to four years. That's your timeline, right? And where is that center? Well, many people is like right in their body over here by the solar plexus, but it could be anywhere. Your future could be up. If your future is down here, I might tell you to raise it up at some point because I want your future to be looking up. These are all the NLP tricks we use to really make things more powerful. To really take what you would do kind of by accident, we apply structure to it and certain language that was invented by a man named Milton Erickson, the world's most famous hypnotist, but again, it's not the plexus. It's just simply using powerful patterns to give you the outcome that will serve you. You're always in control, so remember that. Okay? So with that, it is now time to go into our timeline regression. And when we're done, I won't say you'll be a new person, but you'll certainly be a better version of who you are without all the weight and negativity that comes from being saddled with eliminating beliefs.
